title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
description: Create isolated environment and install all required packages for CleanBP implementation and baseline comparisons. No GPU-dependent packages needed.

steps.step1: Create Python virtual environment: python3 -m venv .venv
steps.step2: Activate environment: source .venv/bin/activate
steps.step3: Install core dependencies: pip install numpy pandas scipy scikit-learn networkx
steps.step4: Install evaluation tools: pip install matplotlib seaborn tabulate
steps.step5: Install HoloClean-compatible packages: pip install torch --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu (CPU-only version)
steps.step6: Verify installation: python -c 'import numpy, pandas, networkx, sklearn; print("All packages installed")'

title: Download and Preprocess Hospital Dataset
description: Download the standard Hospital benchmark dataset (100K records with FD violations), preprocess, and create ground truth for evaluation. Hospital is the primary benchmark for data cleaning research.

steps.step1: Create data directory: mkdir -p data/hospital
steps.step2: Download Hospital dataset from HoloClean GitHub repository: wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/HoloClean/holoclean/master/testdata/hospital.csv
steps.step3: Download clean ground truth version for evaluation
steps.step4: Define FD constraints for Hospital: ZIP -> City, ZIP -> State, ProviderNumber -> HospitalName, ProviderNumber -> Address, etc.
steps.step5: Preprocess: handle missing values, normalize strings, encode categorical variables
steps.step6: Inject controlled errors at 5%, 10%, 15% rates for robustness testing
steps.step7: Split into train/test with fixed random seed (42)
steps.step8: Compute dataset statistics: #tuples, #violations, violation rate, domain sizes

title: Download and Preprocess Adult Census Dataset
description: Prepare Adult Census dataset as a secondary benchmark with different characteristics (different FDs, larger domains).

steps.step1: Download Adult Census from UCI ML Repository: wget https://archive.ics.uci.edu/ml/machine-learning-databases/adult/adult.data
steps.step2: Download adult.names for schema information
steps.step3: Define FDs: education -> education-num, relationship -> sex, workclass -> occupation (approximate)
steps.step4: Preprocess: handle missing values (marked as '?'), normalize
steps.step5: Create dirty version by randomly corrupting 10% of cells in violation-creating patterns

title: Generate Synthetic Scalability Datasets
description: Create synthetic datasets of varying sizes to test scalability claims (linear in violations, not tuples).

steps.step1: Create Python script generate_synthetic.py with parameters: n_tuples, n_violations, n_attributes
steps.step2: Generate datasets: 10K, 50K, 100K, 500K, 1M tuples with controlled violation rates (1%, 5%, 10%)
steps.step3: Each dataset has 5-10 attributes with simple FDs (A->B, C->D, etc.)
steps.step4: Verify FD violation counts match expected values
steps.step5: Store in data/synthetic/ directory

title: Implement Simple Baseline: Minimum Repair (Kolahi & Lakshmanan 2009)
description: Implement the deterministic minimum repair algorithm as a simple baseline. This provides a lower bound on quality but is fast.

steps.step1: Implement FD violation detection using hash-based partitioning
steps.step2: Implement greedy minimum repair: iteratively fix violations with minimum cost (attribute change)
steps.step3: Support different cost models: uniform cost, frequency-based cost
steps.step4: Run on Hospital dataset, record precision, recall, F1, runtime
steps.step5: No random seeds needed (deterministic algorithm)

title: Implement Baseline: Dense Factor Graph with Standard BP
description: Implement belief propagation on a dense factor graph without sparsification. This tests whether our sparsification is actually necessary.

steps.step1: Implement dense factor graph construction: O(n^2) factors for FDs (all tuple pairs with same LHS)
steps.step2: Implement standard loopy belief propagation (sum-product algorithm)
steps.step3: Use uniform message scheduling (no constraint-aware prioritization)
steps.step4: Set max iterations=100, convergence threshold=1e-6
steps.step5: Run on Hospital dataset (subset if too slow), record: F1, runtime, iterations, memory
steps.step6: Run with 3 random seeds: 42, 123, 456 (initialization affects BP)
steps.expected_time: 30 minutes (may be slow due to dense graph)

title: Implement Baseline: ERACER-Style Relational Dependency Networks
description: Re-implement BP with relational dependency networks (RDNs) as used in ERACER. Compare against CleanBP's factor graph approach.

steps.step1: Implement RDN structure: directed graphical model with dependency edges
steps.step2: Implement shrinkage estimation for parameter tying across related tuples
steps.step3: Use similar message passing but on directed structure
steps.step4: Run on Hospital dataset with 3 random seeds
steps.step5: Record: F1, runtime, convergence behavior

title: Implement CleanBP: Violation-Driven Sparsification
description: Implement the core CleanBP system with violation-driven factor graph sparsification.

steps.step1: Implement FD Violation Detector using hash-based partitioning O(n)
steps.step2: Implement sparse factor graph builder: only create factors for violating tuple pairs
steps.step3: Implement loopy belief propagation with message passing
steps.step4: Implement marginal extraction and MAP repair computation
steps.step5: Set hyperparameters: max_iterations=50, convergence_threshold=1e-6, damping=0.5
steps.step6: Run on Hospital dataset with seeds 42, 123, 456
steps.step7: Record: precision, recall, F1, inference_time, iterations_to_converge, memory_usage, n_factors_created

title: Run CleanBP on Adult Census Dataset
description: Test generalization to different dataset with different FDs and characteristics.

steps.step1: Run CleanBP on Adult dataset with same hyperparameters as Hospital
steps.step2: Use seeds 42, 123, 456
steps.step3: Record all metrics: F1, precision, recall, time, memory, iterations
steps.step4: Compare performance characteristics vs Hospital dataset

title: Ablation Study: Impact of Violation-Driven Sparsification
description: Test the impact of sparsification by comparing against partial implementations.

steps.step1: Variant A (Dense): All tuple pairs with same LHS create factors (baseline from Exp 6)
steps.step2: Variant B (Violation-Only): Only violating pairs create factors, but no attribute separation
steps.step3: Variant C (Full CleanBP): Violation-driven + attribute separation
steps.step4: Run all variants on Hospital dataset with seed=42
steps.step5: Compare: F1 score, runtime, memory, number of factors
steps.step7: Verify graph size claim: plot factors vs violations (should be linear)

title: Ablation Study: Impact of Attribute-Separated Factors
description: Test the impact of decomposing multi-attribute FDs into single-attribute factors.

steps.step1: Variant A (Joint Factors): Multi-attribute FDs create joint factors over all RHS attributes
steps.step2: Variant B (Separated Factors): Each RHS attribute gets its own factor (CleanBP approach)
steps.step3: Run both on Hospital dataset with seeds 42, 123, 456
steps.step4: Compare: F1 score (should be within 5%), runtime, convergence iterations
steps.step5: Measure approximation quality: how close are marginals to joint factor version

title: Ablation Study: Impact of Constraint-Aware Message Scheduling
description: Test whether prioritized message scheduling improves convergence speed.

steps.step1: Variant A (Uniform Scheduling): Standard round-robin message passing
steps.step2: Variant B (Violation-Priority): Prioritize messages along violation chains (CleanBP)
steps.step3: Run both on Hospital dataset with seeds 42, 123, 456
steps.step4: Compare: iterations to convergence, total runtime, final F1
steps.step5: Plot convergence curves: marginal change vs iteration

title: Scalability Experiment: Synthetic Datasets
description: Test scalability claims on synthetic datasets of varying sizes.

steps.step1: Run CleanBP on synthetic datasets: 10K, 50K, 100K, 500K, 1M tuples
steps.step2: For each size, test violation rates: 1%, 5%, 10%
steps.step3: Record: inference time, memory usage, number of factors
steps.step4: Fit scaling curves: time vs n_tuples, time vs n_violations
steps.step5: Verify linear scaling with violations (not quadratic with tuples)

title: Uncertainty Calibration Analysis
description: Evaluate quality of uncertainty estimates from CleanBP marginals.

steps.step1: Compute Expected Calibration Error (ECE) for CleanBP marginals
steps.step2: Bin predictions by confidence (10 bins)
steps.step3: For each bin, compare average confidence vs accuracy
steps.step4: Compute Brier score (proper scoring rule)
steps.step5: Compare against baseline: uncertainty from uniform distribution
steps.step6: Plot calibration curve: predicted vs observed accuracy
steps.step7: Run on Hospital dataset with seeds 42, 123, 456

title: Statistical Significance Testing
description: Perform statistical tests to verify results are significant.

steps.step1: Collect F1 scores across 3 seeds for CleanBP and each baseline
steps.step2: Perform paired t-test between CleanBP and each baseline
steps.step3: Report p-values and effect sizes (Cohen's d)
steps.step4: Use significance threshold alpha=0.05
steps.step5: Create summary table: mean ± std for each method, with significance markers

title: Success Criteria Verification
description: Check if all success criteria from the proposal are met.

steps.step1: Criterion 1: Inference time < 10 minutes on Hospital? Check results/cleanbp_full_hospital_*.json
steps.step2: Criterion 2: F1 within 10% of HoloClean? Compare results (HoloClean F1 ~0.85 expected)
steps.step3: Criterion 3: ECE < 0.15? Check results/calibration_hospital_*.json
steps.step4: Criterion 4: Better than ERACER-style BP? Compare F1 scores
steps.step5: Criterion 5: Linear scaling with violations? Check scalability curves
steps.step6: Generate pass/fail report for each criterion

title: Generate Main Results Figure
description: Create bar chart comparing F1 scores and runtimes across all methods.

steps.step1: Load results from all experiments
steps.step2: Create grouped bar chart: F1 score by method (CleanBP, Dense BP, ERACER, Minimum Repair)
steps.step3: Create grouped bar chart: Runtime by method (log scale)
steps.step4: Add error bars showing std across 3 seeds
steps.step5: Add annotations for statistical significance
steps.step6: Save as high-resolution PNG (300 DPI) and PDF for paper

title: Generate Ablation Study Figures
description: Visualize the impact of each CleanBP component.

steps.step1: Create ablation bar chart: F1 vs variant (Dense, Violation-only, Full)
steps.step2: Create speedup chart: Runtime vs variant
steps.step3: Create factors count chart: Number of factors vs variant
steps.step4: Convergence curves: marginal delta vs iteration for scheduling ablation
steps.step5: Save all as high-resolution figures

title: Generate Scalability Figure
description: Visualize scalability claims with proper curves.

steps.step1: Plot inference time vs number of tuples (linear scale)
steps.step2: Plot inference time vs number of violations (should be linear)
steps.step3: Plot number of factors vs violations (verify linear relationship)
steps.step4: Add trend lines with R² values
steps.step5: Highlight the difference between O(n²) and O(m) where m=violations

title: Generate Calibration Figure
description: Visualize uncertainty calibration quality.

steps.step1: Plot calibration curve: predicted confidence vs observed accuracy
steps.step2: Add diagonal line (perfect calibration)
steps.step3: Show ECE value in legend
steps.step4: Create reliability diagram with bin histograms
steps.step5: Save high-resolution figure

title: Generate Summary Tables
description: Create LaTeX-ready tables for paper.

steps.step1: Create main results table: Method | F1 | Precision | Recall | Time (s) | Memory (MB)
steps.step2: Create ablation table: Component | F1 | Time | Speedup
steps.step3: Create scalability table: Dataset Size | Violations | Time | Factors
steps.step4: Export as LaTeX tables and CSV for easy editing

title: Final Results Aggregation and Report
description: Aggregate all results into a comprehensive report.

steps.step1: Load all JSON result files
steps.step2: Compute aggregate statistics: mean, std, min, max across seeds
steps.step3: Identify best and worst performing configurations
steps.step4: Summarize key findings: speedup achieved, accuracy maintained, etc.
steps.step5: Generate final_report.json with all metrics and conclusions
